title: ranunculus purshii var. humifusus
intro: Ranunculus purshii var. humifusus, also known as a variant of the Western buttercup, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and low-growing habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus purshii var. humifusus include its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into a tea, tincture, or salve, which can be applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
